grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements row 232; paper failure mode domestic_violence
grounding_note: Row 232 (adopted nieces, forced visitation, past abuse charges) split humans between Custody+Adoption and Custody+Restraining Orders — abuse-as-context versus abuse-as-requested-relief. The paper found FETCH probed for abuse in only 10% of DV flip cases. Openings describe a custody/parenting conflict; the hidden fact is either qualifying abuse (FAPA restraining order / DV) or its documented absence. Safety-aware: facts are stated plainly, no coaching, no emergency framing.

scenarios.opening_query: The other parent has been causing problems and I need advice about my child. I don't know what to do next.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Restraining Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: Last week he hit me while I was holding our daughter and said he'd come back. I want an order keeping him away from us.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He hit me last week while I was holding our daughter and threatened to come back. I want an order keeping him away.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether there has been violence or threats and whether protective relief is sought
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no violence — he just keeps returning her hours late and skipping his weekends, and I want the schedule enforced.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No violence — he just ignores the schedule, returns her late, skips weekends. I want it enforced.
scenarios.vetting_note: Direct rebuild of the paper's x0041 with a concrete protective-relief request; the abuse disclosure is the entire flip.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex is fighting me over our custody arrangement and things keep escalating.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The escalation is that she slashed my tires and shows up pounding on my door at night — I feel unsafe and want a protective order.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She slashed my tires and pounds on my door at night — I don't feel safe and want a protective order.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the escalation involves threats or violence needing protective orders
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The escalation is legal — she filed to move the kids out of state and I have 30 days to respond.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's escalating in court — she filed to relocate the kids and I have 30 days to respond.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Escalating' hides whether the conflict is physical or procedural; both readings are common.

scenarios.opening_query: I need protection from my child's father. He won't leave us alone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's never been violent or threatening — 'won't leave us alone' means constant texts about wanting more parenting time and threatening to take me to court. I need the custody case handled.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's never been violent — it's constant texts demanding more parenting time and threats to take me to court. I need the custody side handled.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the conduct is abuse requiring protection or a custody dispute in disguise
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He choked me in front of our son two weeks ago; I have photos and a police report and need the order now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He choked me in front of our son two weeks ago — I have photos and a police report. I need the order.
scenarios.vetting_note: Tests the reverse error: protective-order framing that is really a custody matter. Both facts stated plainly.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm divorcing my husband and I'm frightened about how he'll react when the papers arrive.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Restraining Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's threatened violence before when I've talked about leaving, and he controls all the money — I want protective orders in place before he's served.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's threatened violence whenever I've talked about leaving, and he controls all our money. I want protection in place before he's served.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether there is a history of threats or control requiring protective relief alongside the divorce
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's never been threatening — I'm just anxious about the conversation. The real work is the divorce itself.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's never threatened me — I'm just dreading the conversation. It's really about the divorce.
scenarios.vetting_note: Rebuilds x0011 (financial control + threats); fear alone vs qualifying abuse history separates the labels.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter's stepfather is a problem and I want to change our parenting plan because of him.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: CPS opened an investigation after her school reported bruises from his 'discipline' — the agency is now involved with both households.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: CPS opened a case after the school reported bruises from his 'discipline' — the agency's involved with both homes now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether child-welfare authorities have opened a case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No agency is involved — he's just hostile at exchanges and badmouths me to her, and I want the plan adjusted.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No agency involved — he's just hostile at exchanges and badmouths me. I want the plan adjusted.
scenarios.vetting_note: DHS involvement is the taxonomy's own boundary for the Juvenile/DHS label; a fact parents know precisely.

scenarios.opening_query: I adopted my nieces and my ex-partner is forcing visits they're refusing to go to. There's a case number already.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Restraining Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: My ex has two past domestic abuse convictions against me and the behavior is continuing — I want protection for me and the girls, not just a schedule change.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She has two past domestic abuse convictions against me and it's still happening — I want protection for me and the girls, not just a schedule change.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether documented abuse and ongoing conduct call for protective orders beyond the custody case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The old charges were years ago and nothing since — the girls just don't want to go, and the parenting plan needs modifying.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The old charges were years ago, nothing since — the girls just don't want the visits, so the plan needs changing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 232 nearly verbatim: the humans split exactly on abuse-as-context vs abuse-as-relief; both variants authored.

scenarios.opening_query: My son's mother took him and won't tell me where they are. I have court-ordered parenting time.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || International Law > Family Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: Her sister finally told me they flew to her home country — she has family there and no plans to return.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Her sister told me they flew to her home country — she has family there and isn't coming back.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the child was taken across international borders
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're at her mother's place across town — she's just refusing exchanges.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're at her mom's across town — she's just refusing the exchanges.
scenarios.vetting_note: Hague-abduction geography is the fact; same violation reads totally differently across town vs across borders.

scenarios.opening_query: I want full custody of my kids. Their mother's household is not a safe environment anymore.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: DHS already removed the kids from her home last Friday — they're in emergency foster care, and I'm fighting to have them placed with me instead.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: DHS removed them from her home Friday — they're in emergency foster care and I want them placed with me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the children are in state custody
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No agency has stepped in — I'm going by what the kids tell me, and I want the custody case to deal with it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No agency involved — I'm going on what the kids tell me and want it handled in the custody case.
scenarios.vetting_note: Foster-care placement moves the fight from private custody court to the dependency system; a hard procedural fact.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex violated our custody order again this weekend and I'm done letting it slide.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: This time the DA charged him with custodial interference — there's a criminal case now and I'm the named victim witness.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The DA charged him with custodial interference this time — it's a criminal case and I'm the victim witness.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the violation has become a criminal prosecution
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No charges — I want to file for contempt and get the order enforced with makeup time.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No charges — I want contempt filed and makeup parenting time.
scenarios.vetting_note: Civil enforcement vs parallel criminal case; the filed-charge fact reroutes the immediate need.

scenarios.opening_query: I need a restraining order against my ex-boyfriend. The situation is getting worse every week.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: We dated briefly two years ago but never lived together — the advocate said that means the family-abuse order doesn't fit and I need the stalking-order kind instead.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We only dated briefly and never lived together — the advocate said the family-abuse order doesn't apply, so I need the stalking order instead.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the relationship qualifies for a FAPA order or requires a stalking order
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We lived together for three years and share a lease — the family-abuse order applies to us.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We lived together three years and share a lease — so the family-abuse order applies.
scenarios.vetting_note: FAPA's household-member requirement is the statutory boundary the paper's criminal_vs_restraining stratum kept missing; relationship history is a real disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: My children's grandmother is interfering with how I raise them and now she's threatening legal action.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Grandparent/Third Party Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: She actually filed a petition for court-ordered grandparent visitation last week — I was served yesterday.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She filed for court-ordered grandparent visitation — I was served yesterday.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a third-party visitation petition has been filed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's filed — it's tangled up in my custody case with their dad because she babysits during his time.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's filed — it's really part of my custody case with their dad since she babysits during his time.
scenarios.vetting_note: Third-party petition is a distinct statutory action; the served-papers fact makes it concrete.

scenarios.opening_query: I've been raising my sister's baby for a year and now she wants him back. I'm all he knows.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Guardianship || Family Law > Adoption/DHS || Family Law > Grandparent/Third Party Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: DHS placed him with me as a foster resource after her termination case started — the state is moving toward adoption and I want to be the one who adopts him.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: DHS placed him with me during her termination case — the state's moving toward adoption and I want to adopt him.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the child is in state care with adoption proceedings versus an informal family arrangement
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's informal — she left him with me and no court or agency is involved; I need legal guardianship so I can enroll him in daycare and make medical decisions.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's all informal — no court, no agency. I need guardianship so I can handle school and doctors.
scenarios.vetting_note: State-involvement fact selects among guardianship, adoption, and DHS routes — three genuinely adjacent labels.

scenarios.opening_query: The father of my baby refuses to help with anything and denies she's even his.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Support/Modification || Family Law > Paternity
scenarios.hidden_fact: We were never married and he's not on the birth certificate — no legal parentage exists yet, which I'm told has to come first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We were never married and he's not on the birth certificate — so legal paternity has to be established first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether legal parentage has been established
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He signed the paternity acknowledgment at the hospital — he's legally her father; he just won't pay.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He signed the acknowledgment at the hospital — he's legally her dad, he just won't pay.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 234's cluster: acknowledgment status is the gateway fact between paternity and support.

scenarios.opening_query: I pay child support every month but I haven't been allowed to see my kids since March.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Paternity
scenarios.hidden_fact: We were never married and I never established legal paternity — the support was a voluntary arrangement, and apparently I have no enforceable visitation rights until parentage is legal.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We were never married and paternity was never established — my support's been voluntary, and I apparently have no enforceable rights until it's legal.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the paying parent has established legal parentage
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's a full court order from our divorce covering support and my parenting time — she's violating it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: There's a court order from our divorce covering everything — she's just violating it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Fathers routinely discover the parentage gap only at intake; the order-vs-informal fact routes it.

scenarios.opening_query: My 16-year-old wants to live on her own and honestly our home situation is complicated.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Emancipation
scenarios.hidden_fact: She has a full-time job, her own apartment lined up, and wants to be legally emancipated — she's asking me to support the petition.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She has a full-time job and an apartment lined up — she wants legal emancipation and asked me to support the petition.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the minor seeks formal emancipation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She just wants to live with her dad full-time — it's really about changing the custody schedule.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She really just wants to live with her dad full-time — it's a custody-schedule change.
scenarios.vetting_note: Emancipation is rare but has a dedicated label; self-supporting facts are the statutory test.

scenarios.opening_query: My stepdaughter's biological father hasn't been in her life for years and we want to make things official.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Adoption/DHS || Family Law > Terminate Parent Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's refusing to consent — we're told his rights have to be involuntarily terminated for abandonment before any adoption can happen.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He won't consent — so his rights have to be terminated for abandonment before the adoption can go forward.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the absent parent consents or termination must be litigated
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's agreed to sign the consent — it's a straightforward stepparent adoption now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He agreed to sign — it's a straightforward stepparent adoption.
scenarios.vetting_note: Consent status splits the friendly adoption from contested termination; the taxonomy separates them.
